commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d505fe72d8e82880c3f19591577fa6390b2ea42c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core and the memory host SDK so QMD-backed `memory_search` can derive a longer manager deadline, bounds one-shot QMD cleanup, forwards search-mode timeout options, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +304, Tests +384. Total +688 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main and v2026.6.10 still wrap `memory_search` in a fixed 15s deadline while the QMD command paths and linked report use `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` for longer QMD searches.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR has a P1 code defect plus contributor proof and maintainer contract/availability decisions, so it is not a safe repair queue item.

target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout and bound one-shot CLI cleanup
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`memory_search` had a fixed 15s outer deadline, but QMD-backed searches can legitimately take longer when `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` is raised. The old outer guard could time out the tool before QMD finished a configured search.

Fixes #91947.

## Summary

- Honors configured QMD search timeout instead of forcing QMD-backed `memory_search` through the historical 15s outer guard.
- Keeps the default `memory_search` deadline at 15s unless the active QMD manager reports a longer whole-search budget.
- Preserves QMD `AbortSignal` subprocess cancellation behavior from #93394 after the rebase.
- Bounds one-shot CLI QMD manager cleanup so pending update work cannot hold tool return indefinitely.
- Threads `qmdSearchModeOverride` through the cached QMD manager deadline path so the budget matches the actual search mode.

## User Impact

QMD-backed `memory_search` can now complete when its configured QMD search timeout is intentionally raised above the old 15s guard.

Default and short-timeout QMD users keep fast failure behavior: if the configured QMD search command timeout is 15s or less, `memory_search` stays on the old 15s guard. When QMD opts into a longer whole-search budget, the initial search, zero-hit forced sync, and retry share one manager deadline instead of each getting a fresh long timeout.

Non-QMD behavior stays bounded by the default guard. Runtime loading, manager setup, wiki supplements, builtin searches, and builtin forced-sync retry share the existing 15s deadline. If QMD fails and the wrapper falls back to the builtin index during the same call, that fallback search has its own 15s cap instead of inheriting the longer QMD budget.

One-shot CLI cleanup is bounded: `QmdMemoryManager.close()` accepts an optional timeout so that timed-out forced sync/update work cannot delay CLI shutdown past a 5s cleanup grace period.

No storage migration or data model change is required.

## Real behavior proof

This is redacted terminal output from a live QMD-backed harness run, not a mock or fake-timer Vitest test. The proof command used the repo-local development entrypoint, created a temporary QMD workspace, wrapped the real `npx -y @tobilu/qmd` CLI so search-like commands slept for 16s, and then invoked `memory_search` through the actual memory-core tool path.

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
9104253dabcb2d4f4b101f51e672edb81a8e2b15

$ git branch --show-current
fix/memory-search-timeout-config

$ node --version
v26.0.0

$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development /tmp/qmd-live-delay-proof.ts
[proof] repo=/home/rev/projects/openclaw
[proof] workspace=/tmp/openclaw-qmd-live-proof-REDACTED
[proof] memory files:
  MEMORY.md
  memory/qmd-proof.md
[proof] backend=qmd
[proof] memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs=60000
[proof] memory.qmd.searchMode=search
[proof] old memory_search outer deadline=15000ms
[proof] qmd wrapper=/tmp/openclaw-qmd-live-proof-REDACTED/qmd-delay-wrapper
[proof] qmd wrapper behavior=sleep 16000ms only for search/query/vsearch, then exec npx -y @tobilu/qmd
[proof] running memory_search for unique token: qmd-proof-live-delay-token
[qmd-delay-wrapper] delayed search command by 16000ms
[proof] memory_search completed after old 15s boundary
{
  "configuredTimeoutMs": 60000,
  "oldOuterDeadlineMs": 15000,
  "elapsedMs": 28878,
  "disabled": false,
  "debug": {
    "backend": "qmd",
    "configuredMode": "search",
    "effectiveMode": "search",
    "searchMs": 22653,
    "hits": 1
  },
  "resultCount": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/qmd-proof.md",
      "source": "memory",
      "corpus": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
[proof] PASS elapsedMs=28878 > oldOuterDeadlineMs=15000
[proof] PASS resultCount=1
[proof] PASS first hit path=memory/qmd-proof.md
```

Why this proves the behavior:

- The harness used a live QMD-backed `memory_search`, not a mocked manager.
- The QMD command path was intentionally delayed by more than the old fixed 15s outer guard.
- The call returned after `28878ms`, which is beyond the old `15000ms` deadline.
- The result included a real QMD memory hit from `memory/qmd-proof.md`.
- The configured QMD timeout was `60000ms`, showing the PR honors a raised QMD search budget instead of failing at the historical 15s wrapper.

## Review findings addressed

- ClawSweeper fallback deadline finding: QMD-to-builtin fallback now has a bounded 15s fallback search deadline.
- ClawSweeper cached-wrapper finding: `qmdSearchModeOverride` is now forwarded through `FallbackMemoryManager.getSearchTimeoutMs(opts)` so cached full-QMD managers compute the same budget as the actual per-call search mode.
- ClawSweeper cancellation overlap finding: this PR preserves the QMD subprocess cancellation behavior from #93394 and does not re-own that work.
- Rank-up move: the initial QMD search, zero-hit forced sync, and retry share one whole-search deadline.
- Rank-up move: one-shot CLI QMD forced-sync cleanup is bounded by passing a 5s cleanup grace period into manager close.
- Availability tradeoff: default/short QMD timeout configs stay on the old 15s `memory_search` guard; longer waits require a QMD search timeout above 15s and then apply to one shared whole-search deadline.

## Validation

Latest focused validation run for this PR line:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
  ✓ 26 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts
  ✓ passed locally before the final cached-wrapper regression patch; CI/checks should refresh this on the final head

CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "timeout|abort|close" --reporter=verbose
  ✓ 17 passed | 133 skipped

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts
  ✓ 20 passed

pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
  ✓ completed without errors before the final cached-wrapper regression patch

git diff --check
  ✓ completed without output before the final cached-wrapper regression patch
```

Validation commands:

- `CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "timeout|abort|close" --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `git diff --check`

## Risk Before Merge

- [P1] Configured QMD searches can intentionally keep an interactive `memory_search` call running far longer than the former 15s guard, so maintainers should accept that availability tradeoff explicitly.
- [P1] The optional `MemorySearchManager.getSearchTimeoutMs` method and optional `close(timeoutMs)` lifecycle parameter expand the memory-host SDK/interface surface. The additions are optional, but the permanent contract direction needs maintainer acceptance.
- [P1] QMD subprocess cancellation remains covered by #93394, so this PR should not be treated as closing separate orphaned-process work.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

[proof] PASS elapsedMs=28878 > oldOuterDeadlineMs=15000
